title: Failed hlstool with specific function.
body:
Hi! I'm playing around with the hlstool within this project. But I got a weird error.

I'm trying to HLS a Gaussian Filter function within CIRCT.

```
#include <ctype.h>
#include <math.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>

#define COLS      20
#define ROWS      20
#define KERN_SIZE 3
#define KERN_RAD  (KERN_SIZE >> 1)
#define PIXELS    COLS* ROWS
#define MAX(x, y) (((x) > (y)) ? (x) : (y))
#define MIN(x, y) (((x) < (y)) ? (x) : (y))

void gauss(uint8_t input[PIXELS], uint8_t output[PIXELS])
{
    // Gauss kernel
    int16_t kernel[KERN_SIZE * KERN_SIZE] = {1, 2, 1, 2, 4, 2, 1, 2, 1};

    for (int32_t y = 0; y < ROWS; y++) {
        for (int32_t x = 0; x < COLS; x++) {
            // temp var to store the result
            int16_t sum = 0;
            for (int32_t i = -KERN_RAD; i <= KERN_RAD; i++) {
                for (int32_t j = -KERN_RAD; j <= KERN_RAD; j++) {
                    int32_t yi = y + i;
                    int32_t xj = x + j;
                    if (xj >= 0 && xj < COLS && yi >= 0 && yi < ROWS)
                        sum +=
                            (int16_t)(input[yi * COLS + xj] * kernel[(i + KERN_RAD) * KERN_SIZE + j + KERN_RAD]);
                }
            }
            // Normalize and store
            output[y * COLS + x] = (uint8_t)(MAX(MIN(255, sum >> 4), 0));
        }
    }
}
```

I firstly use `Polygeist` to translate the above C code into MLIR as following:

```
module {
  func.func @gauss(%arg0: memref<400xi8>, %arg1: memref<400xi8>) {
    %c2 = arith.constant 2 : index
    %c-1 = arith.constant -1 : index
    %c20 = arith.constant 20 : index
    %c0 = arith.constant 0 : index
    %c1 = arith.constant 1 : index
    %true = arith.constant true
    %c4_i32 = arith.constant 4 : i32
    %c255_i32 = arith.constant 255 : i32
    %c1_i32 = arith.constant 1 : i32
    %c3_i32 = arith.constant 3 : i32
    %c0_i16 = arith.constant 0 : i16
    %c20_i32 = arith.constant 20 : i32
    %c0_i32 = arith.constant 0 : i32
    %c4_i16 = arith.constant 4 : i16
    %c2_i16 = arith.constant 2 : i16
    %c1_i16 = arith.constant 1 : i16
    %alloca = memref.alloca() : memref<9xi16>
    affine.store %c1_i16, %alloca[0] : memref<9xi16>
    affine.store %c2_i16, %alloca[1] : memref<9xi16>
    affine.store %c1_i16, %alloca[2] : memref<9xi16>
    affine.store %c2_i16, %alloca[3] : memref<9xi16>
    affine.store %c4_i16, %alloca[4] : memref<9xi16>
    affine.store %c2_i16, %alloca[5] : memref<9xi16>
    affine.store %c1_i16, %alloca[6] : memref<9xi16>
    affine.store %c2_i16, %alloca[7] : memref<9xi16>
    affine.store %c1_i16, %alloca[8] : memref<9xi16>
    scf.for %arg2 = %c0 to %c20 step %c1 {
      %0 = arith.index_cast %arg2 : index to i32
      %1 = arith.muli %0, %c20_i32 : i32
      scf.for %arg3 = %c0 to %c20 step %c1 {
        %2 = arith.index_cast %arg3 : index to i32
        %3 = scf.for %arg4 = %c-1 to %c2 step %c1 iter_args(%arg5 = %c0_i16) -> (i16) {
          %12 = arith.index_cast %arg4 : index to i32
          %13 = arith.addi %0, %12 : i32
          %14 = arith.cmpi sge, %13, %c0_i32 : i32
          %15 = arith.cmpi slt, %13, %c20_i32 : i32
          %16 = arith.andi %14, %15 : i1
          %17 = arith.muli %13, %c20_i32 : i32
          %18 = arith.addi %12, %c1_i32 : i32
          %19 = arith.muli %18, %c3_i32 : i32
          %20 = scf.for %arg6 = %c-1 to %c2 step %c1 iter_args(%arg7 = %arg5) -> (i16) {
            %21 = arith.index_cast %arg6 : index to i32
            %22 = arith.addi %2, %21 : i32
            %23 = arith.cmpi sge, %22, %c0_i32 : i32
            %24 = arith.cmpi slt, %22, %c20_i32 : i32
            %25 = arith.andi %24, %16 : i1
            %26 = arith.andi %23, %25 : i1
            %27 = scf.if %26 -> (i16) {
              %28 = arith.addi %17, %22 : i32
              %29 = arith.index_cast %28 : i32 to index
              %30 = memref.load %arg0[%29] : memref<400xi8>
              %31 = arith.extsi %30 : i8 to i32
              %32 = arith.addi %19, %21 : i32
              %33 = arith.addi %32, %c1_i32 : i32
              %34 = arith.index_cast %33 : i32 to index
              %35 = memref.load %alloca[%34] : memref<9xi16>
              %36 = arith.extsi %35 : i16 to i32
              %37 = arith.muli %31, %36 : i32
              %38 = arith.trunci %37 : i32 to i16
              %39 = arith.addi %arg7, %38 : i16
              scf.yield %39 : i16
            } else {
              scf.yield %arg7 : i16
            }
            scf.yield %27 : i16
          }
          scf.yield %20 : i16
        }
        %4 = arith.addi %1, %2 : i32
        %5 = arith.index_cast %4 : i32 to index
        %6 = arith.extsi %3 : i16 to i32
        %7 = arith.shrsi %6, %c4_i32 : i32
        %8 = arith.cmpi sgt, %7, %c255_i32 : i32
        %result = scf.if %8 -> (i32) {
          scf.yield %c255_i32 : i32
        } else {
          scf.yield %7 : i32
        }
        %9 = scf.if %8 -> (i1) {
          scf.yield %true : i1
        } else {
          %12 = arith.cmpi sgt, %7, %c0_i32 : i32
          scf.yield %12 : i1
        }
        %10 = scf.if %9 -> (i32) {
          // %12 = arith.select %8, %c255_i32, %7 : i32
          // scf.yield %12 : i32
          scf.yield %result : i32
        } else {
          scf.yield %c0_i32 : i32
        }
        %11 = arith.trunci %10 : i32 to i8
        memref.store %11, %arg1[%5] : memref<400xi8>
      }
    }
    return
  }
}
```

Then I use the `hlstool` as:

```
hlstool gauss.mlir -dynamic-hw
```

Then the error showed up:

```
hlstool: /home/bi/Desktop/circt/llvm/mlir/lib/IR/PatternMatch.cpp:276: virtual void mlir::RewriterBase::eraseOp(mlir::Operation *): Assertion `op->use_empty() && "expected 'op' to have no uses"' failed.
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/circt and include the crash backtrace.
Stack dump:
0.	Program arguments: /home/bi/Desktop/circt/build/bin/hlstool gauss.mlir -dynamic-hw
 #0 0x00005600d347eb1d llvm::sys::PrintStackTrace(llvm::raw_ostream&, int) /home/bi/Desktop/circt/llvm/llvm/lib/Support/Unix/Signals.inc:567:11
```

Which I've asked about once on discourse, this should be a bug somewhere in the lowering.
